The Court accepted the proof of servce flied by Mr. Worth and directed him to use form DE120 in the future. The Court stated that there were no orignal bank statements filed, and that the bond needed to be increased. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jewell Hargleroad (Counsel for Wendy Hermann, Petitioner); Seth Skootsky (Counsel for Susan David, Objector); Wendy Hermann (Petitioner).

The Court and counsel discussed the procedural status of the case at some length, concluding that the amended Petition superceded the October 2005 Petition and that the will contest was otherwise at issue.

After extensive discussion as to the merits of the appointment of a special administrator and as to how procedurally that appointment should transpire (i.e., by new, separate ex parte application), the Court continued the hearing on all pending matters to April 16, 2007 at 11:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 so as to allow sufficient time to review the results of the pending Marin County proceedings.
